title: Jerne
content:
{{Top|fa|leg}} {{Otheruses}} {{CelestialBody |image=[[File:JerneSystem-DKD.png]] |name=Jerne |region=*[[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' — Based on corresponding data for [[Jerne system/Legends|Jerne system]] *[[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] |sector=[[Kanz sector/Legends|Kanz sector]]{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Jerne system/Legends|Jerne system]] |system=[[Jerne system/Legends|Jerne system]] |coordinates=N-4 |xyz= |distance= |suns= |orbited= |position= |moons=2{{StoryCite|story=Dark Knight's Devilry|book=The Empire Strikes Back Monthly 153}} |routes= |lengthday= |lengthyear= |class=Terrestrial |diameter= |atmosphere= |climate= |gravity= |terrain=[[Desert/Legends|Desert]] |water= |interest=[[Adony Station]] |flora= |fauna= |otherlife= |species=[[Jerni]] {{C|extinct}} |otherspecies=[[Human/Legends|Humans]] |socialgroup= |language= |government= |population= |demonym= |cities=[[Jerne City H.Q.]] |imports= |exports= |affiliation=[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] }} {{Quote|So that's why we came to Jerne… to see if the crystal is still in a vault near Adony Station.|Luke Skywalker|}} '''Jerne''' was a [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories']] [[Kanz sector/Legends|Kanz sector]]. Its [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] [[Jerni]] [[Species/Legends|species]] had gone extinct by the time the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] militarized the world and headquartered themselves in [[Jerne City H.Q.]], an event that had occurred recently by [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. At that point, the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] crafted a trap for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]—planted [[Image tape|datatapes]] claimed that a highly valuable artifact called the [[Eternity Crystal]] with the power to [[Time travel/Legends|wind back]] [[Time/Legends|time]] was located on Jerne—ensnaring fellow Rebel [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] in it as well. Allured, the Rebels [[Mission to Jerne|traveled]] there in search of the [[Crystal/Legends|crystal]] but were shot down by Imperials. After crashing and being captured by the Empire, the Rebels were captured again, this time by a new group: a local band of [[Meeka Reen's pirates|guerrillas]] led by a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] named [[Meeka Reen]]. The [[Guerrilla warfare/Legends|guerrillas]] were very interested in Skywalker and Organa's presence and convinced them to explain why they had come to Jerne, resulting in Reen pretending to make a deal with the Rebels so that she could acquire the crystal for herself. The guerrillas and Rebels made their way to the Eternity Crystal's supposed location, [[Adony Station]], where—following a short skirmish with Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]—Reen betrayed the Rebels, entered the ruins that Organa recognized from the [[Data-tape/Legends|datatapes]], and was subsequently blown up by Vader's planted [[Bomb/Legends|explosives]]. Skywalker and Organa survived the ordeal, escaping the [[Jerne system/Legends|Jerne system]] on an Imperial [[TIE/sh VIP shuttle|scout ship]]. ==Description== Jerne was a [[Desert/Legends|desert]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] located in the [[Kanz sector/Legends|Kanz sector's]] [[Jerne system/Legends|Jerne system]] in the [[New Territories/Legends|New Territories]] portion of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. It was [[Orbit/Legends|orbited]] by two [[Moon/Legends|moons]], whose light made the sand dunes of Jerne sparkle. ==History== ===Early history and a trap=== {{Quote|It had been built by the original inhabitants, the Jerni.|Luke Skywalker, on the discovered starship|}} [[File:JerniRuins-DKD.png|thumb|right|300px|The remains of Jerni society on Jerne]] The desert world of Jerne was originally the [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] of the [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Jerni]] [[Species/Legends|species]]; however, by [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],In "[[Dark Knight's Devilry]]," which depicts the [[mission to Jerne]], [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] wields his [[Anakin Skywalker's second lightsaber/Legends|father's lightsaber]], which he acquires during the events of {{Film|IV}} and loses during those of {{Film|V}}. Per ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'', the [[Time/Legends|time]] period between the events of those two films corresponds to between [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]. [[Millennium/Legends|millennia]] had passed since the Jerni populated the planet, and a former Jerne empire had become forgotten by most. [[Human/Legends|Humans]] [[Colonist/Legends|colonized]] the world after the Jerni's demise, using it until its near destruction.{{CSWECite|II|158|Jerne}} By that point, the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] had recently militarized the world, sparking a slew of rebellious activity. Between that [[Standard year/Legends|year]] and [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] devised a plot to lure the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] to Jerne in an attempt to [[Death/Legends|kill]] her: he planted [[Image tape|datatapes]] in a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] for the Rebels to discover that pointed to a [[Time/Legends|time]]-[[Time travel/Legends|controlling]], history-changing Jerni device called an [[Eternity Crystal]]. Unbeknown to all but Vader, the crystal did not in fact exist and was a mere illusion created by the Sith Lord to lure in the Rebels. The Rebel [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] found the ship, and after Rebel [[Scientist/Legends|scientists]] were able to figure out how to play the tapes, Skywalker fell into Vader's trap by traveling to Jerne with Organa a [[Standard month/Legends|month]] later. ===A Rebel disturbance=== {{Quote|Adony Station? What do you want to go to that rat hole for? There's nothing there but an Imperial outpost and some crumbling old ruins… not even a cantina.|Meeka Reen, to Luke Skywalker|}} [[File:Meeka1.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Meeka Reen holds an audience with the Rebels.]] When the Rebels [[Mission to Jerne|arrived]] in the [[Star system/Legends|system]], they ran into several [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE fighters]] which, much to their surprise, shot them down, causing them to crash on Jerne's surface. After the Imperials loaded Skywalker and Organa onto their [[Reconnaissance Troop Transporter]], however, they were quickly ambushed by [[Meeka Reen's pirates|guerrillas]], who opened fire on the Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. While the Imperial [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Ryko Vant]] was able to flee, the [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]] took out all of the [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] and captured the two Rebels and their [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|droid]], [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]]. The [[Guerrilla warfare/Legends|guerrillas]] took their captives back to their leader, [[Meeka Reen]], who was more concerned with the whereabouts of Vant than the [[Prisoner/Legends|prisoners]]. Vant had used a [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpack]] to fly over to [[Jerne City H.Q.]], where Jerne's Imperial [[Imperial Military/Legends|military]] center was located. The captain entered his office only to find the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Vader waiting for him, extremely disappointed in his work; although the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] was surprised that Vant had captured the Rebels, he had expected their ultimate escape. Vader then brushed off Vant's plans to find the Rebels' location, having already known their ultimate goal: [[Adony Station]]. ===A crystal for all of eternity=== {{Quote|What about Vader? Do you think he's dead too?''"
"''Maybe… with all that armor, you can't be sure. And I, for one, don't feel like hanging around to find out.|Leia Organa and Luke Skywalker|}} [[File:Meeka3.jpg|thumb|right|290px|Luke Skywalker and Meeka Reen discuss the Rebels' presence on Jerne.]] Meanwhile, Skywalker was telling Reen about his plans; the pirate leader was surprised, unsure why anyone would have had interest in Adony Station. It was then that a subordinate of Reen's, [[Skutch]], discovered that R2-D2 had an [[Homing beacon/Legends|electronic trace]] attached to him, forcing Skywalker to hurry up with his story and tell Reen the truth about the ship and [[Data-tape/Legends|datatapes]] he had discovered as well as the Eternity Crystal they were after. Reen expressed skepticism of their story but was inwardly extremely interested in the power that the Eternity Crystal could grant her. Reen's plan was to let Skywalker and Organa lead the way, intending to kill them either once they found the crystal or if they were found to be lying and it did not exist. The next [[Local day/Legends|day]], having gathered supplies, the guerrillas and Rebels traveled to Adony Station, quickly noticing its lack of reinforcements. Despite its emptiness, Reen and Skywalker both felt uneasy about the operation. The Imperials that were there opened fire once they realized they had invaders, and following a fierce battle in which Reen lost six men, all of the stormtroopers were defeated. Recognizing the location from the image tapes, Organa took charge, pointing out the entrance to the ruins where she believed the Eternity Crystal laid. [[File:ByeByeJerne-DKD.jpg|thumb|left|290px|The Rebels fly away from Jerne.]] Reen then betrayed the Rebels, ordering Skutch to make sure that they were far away enough to stay out of her way. She used a [[Grenade/Legends|grenade]] to open the ruins, but a [[Bomb/Legends|bomb]] that had been planted by Vader then detonated, killing Reen and her associates and covering the Rebels with rubble. Just as Vader and Vant arrived to inspect the remains, Skywalker used his [[Anakin Skywalker's second lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] to cut himself and Organa out. Distracted while talking with Vant about his masterplan, Vader failed to notice the Rebels' escape, allowing them to throw a [[Proton grenade/Legends|proton grenade]] at the Imperials. The explosion killed Vant and left Vader alive, but it stunned him long enough to let the Rebels depart on an Imperial [[TIE/sh VIP shuttle|scout ship]]. ==Inhabitants== {{Main|Jerni|Meeka Reen's pirates}} {{Quote|The leader of this rat pack is a not-so-nice young lady called… Meeka Reen.|Leia Organa|}} Millennia before 0 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]], the Jerni had an empire on Jerne. Only a few ruins remained of it, however, and Human colonists came to use the planet, nearly destroying it. By that time, a group of Human guerrillas led by Meeka Reen became prominent on the planet. Also residing on the world were Imperial stormtroopers and [[Imperial officer/Legends|officers]]. A [[Writing/Legends|written]] and [[Language/Legends|spoken]] [[Jerne (language)|language]] shared its name with the planet.{{GamerCite|9|Race for the Tessent}} ==Locations== {{Quote|No one's going anywhere ''near'' Adony Station until I know exactly what we're after.|Meeka Reen|}} Meeka Reen and her pirates were headquartered in a [[Cave/Legends|cave]]-like location within a [[Mountain/Legends|mountain]]. The Imperials on the planet were based in Jerne City H.Q., which was comprised of a few buildings and defenses. In addition to the [[City/Legends|city]], a small Imperial outpost was located at Adony Station, which contained little reinforcements so as to lure in the Rebels. Adony Station was later destroyed, leaving behind only a large crater in its place. ==Behind the scenes== Jerne first appeared in the [[Star Wars comic books|comic story]] "[[Dark Knight's Devilry]]," which was written by [[Steve Moore]], illustrated by [[Alan Davis]], and first published by [[Marvel Comics]] in the [[The Empire Strikes Back Monthly 153|153th issue]] of ''[[The Empire Strikes Back Monthly]]'' in [[January]] [[1982]].''[[Star Wars Legends Epic Collection: The Original Marvel Years Vol. 3]]'' The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Jerne system, and therefore the planet itself, in grid square N-4. ==Appearances== *{{StoryCite|story=Dark Knight's Devilry|book=The Empire Strikes Back Monthly 153|reprint=1}} {{1st}} ==Sources== *{{GalaxyCite|11|ComicScan|ComicScan: Paint Him Black}} {{Po}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' *''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' *{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Jerne}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |es=Jerne }} [[Category:Desert planets]] [[Category:Galactic Empire planets]] [[Category:Kanz sector locations]] [[Category:Planets in the New Territories]] [[Category:Planets in the Outer Rim Territories]]
